description: Generic schema for all ShopifyQL query streams. ShopifyQL returns every column as a string regardless of the underlying data type (numbers, dates, booleans). Casting to the correct type is the responsibility of the downstream dbt model or transformation layer. The 'properties' block below lists the columns from the default sales query as a reference. Any column not listed here is still accepted because 'additionalProperties' is open — you will not need to edit this schema when you add new queries or change the SHOW clause.

day.description: The date for this row in ISO-8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Present when the query uses a TIMESERIES clause. Null on TOTALS summary rows.

orders.description: Number of orders for this period. Returned as a string — cast to integer downstream.

gross_sales.description: Total gross sales before discounts, returns, or taxes. Returned as a string — cast to numeric downstream.

discounts.description: Total discount amount applied. Returned as a string — cast to numeric downstream.

returns.description: Total value of returned items. Returned as a string — cast to numeric downstream.

net_sales.description: Gross sales minus discounts and returns. Returned as a string — cast to numeric downstream.

shipping_charges.description: Total shipping revenue collected. Returned as a string — cast to numeric downstream.

duties.description: Total duties charged. Returned as a string — cast to numeric downstream.

additional_fees.description: Any additional fees collected. Returned as a string — cast to numeric downstream.

taxes.description: Total taxes collected. Returned as a string — cast to numeric downstream.

total_sales.description: Net sales plus shipping, duties, fees, and taxes. This is the headline figure shown in the Shopify Analytics UI. Returned as a string — cast to numeric downstream.
